steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$f \left(x\right)= \left(x-44\right) \left(x-46\right)$$Graph it and use the visible intercept, root, or vertex the question asks for. The requested value is $45$.

steps.title: Use the intercept shortcut
steps.content: $f(x)=(x-44)(x-46)$ has zeros at $x=44$ and $x=46$. Because the coefficient of $x^2$ is positive, the parabola opens upward, so its minimum occurs at the vertex.

steps.title: Find the midpoint of the zeros
steps.content: For a factored upward-opening parabola, the vertex lies halfway between the two $x$-intercepts: $$\frac{44+46}{2}=45.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The answer $45$ is correct because the minimum occurs at $x=45$. The values $46$ and $44$ are intercepts, not the minimum point. The value $-1$ is the minimum function value, $f(45)=-1$, not the requested $x$-value.
